314,1012,1383,1012,1383,1040,314,1040,Figure 3. Distributions showing the diverse set of languages and domains represented in C OMMON F ORMS .
195,1101,817,1101,817,1134,195,1134,We split the data into a training, validation, and test set.
161,1134,817,1134,817,1166,161,1166,We split the train set by document, rather than page, to en-
161,1166,817,1166,817,1201,161,1201,sure that similar pages used in training do not leak into the
161,1201,817,1201,817,1233,161,1233,validation and test sets. We build an 8k page validation set
161,1233,817,1233,817,1266,161,1266,and a 25k page test set, reserving the rest of the documents
161,1266,294,1266,294,1300,161,1300,for training.
161,1325,525,1325,525,1365,161,1365,3.2. Annotation Consistency
161,1378,817,1378,817,1413,161,1413,Forms from the Web are not consistently annotated. De-
161,1413,817,1413,817,1445,161,1445,spite extensive ﬁltering that reduces the candidate set of
161,1445,817,1445,817,1478,161,1478,documents by more than 99%, there are still annotation in-
161,1478,817,1478,817,1512,161,1512,consistencies in the prepared forms. These can negatively
161,1512,817,1512,817,1544,161,1544,impact the real-world performance of any models trained
161,1544,817,1544,817,1579,161,1579,on C OMMON F ORMS . We provide a representative, but not
161,1579,758,1579,758,1611,161,1611,exhaustive, catalog of such inconsistencies in Table 1 .
195,1613,817,1613,817,1648,195,1648,Some of these arise from unconventional or incorrect use
161,1648,817,1648,817,1680,161,1680,of form elements. A common pattern is to see form ﬁelds
161,1680,817,1680,817,1713,161,1713,used as headers and footers in a document. The automati-
161,1713,817,1713,817,1747,161,1747,cally prepared forms can suffer from misleading heuristics,
161,1747,817,1747,817,1779,161,1779,which often look for straight horizontal lines where form
161,1779,817,1779,817,1814,161,1814,ﬁelds would likely be placed. This leads to the inclusion
161,1814,817,1814,817,1846,161,1846,of spurious form ﬁelds. Text ﬁelds can be used in place of
161,1846,817,1846,817,1879,161,1879,signature ﬁelds, or signature ﬁelds are left blank, intended
161,1879,705,1879,705,1913,161,1913,for a wet signature rather than a digital signature.
195,1915,817,1915,817,1947,195,1947,However, some of these are related to the semantics of
161,1947,817,1947,817,1982,161,1982,the form itself. “For Ofﬁcial Use Only” sections are some-
880,1101,1265,1101,1265,1134,880,1134,times ﬁllable and sometimes not.
1290,1101,1536,1101,1536,1134,1290,1134,Similarly, forms with
880,1134,1536,1134,1536,1166,880,1166,“Circle All that Apply” sections are only occasionally in-
880,1166,1436,1166,1436,1201,880,1201,teractive, even if the rest of the form is interactive.
912,1203,1536,1203,1536,1235,912,1235,Qualitative results show that despite these inconsisten-
880,1235,1536,1235,1536,1269,880,1269,cies, models trained on C OMMON F ORMS are eminently
880,1269,1536,1269,1536,1302,880,1302,useful, even on complex forms. These systematic incon-
880,1302,1536,1302,1536,1336,880,1336,sistencies provide an adverse training signal, and are a con-
880,1336,1536,1336,1536,1369,880,1369,sequence of using scraped forms rather than manually an-
880,1369,1536,1369,1536,1401,880,1401,notating a dataset. However, they are a trade-off made to
880,1401,1536,1401,1536,1436,880,1436,scale the dataset to a practical size. We do not attempt to
880,1436,1536,1436,1536,1468,880,1468,ﬁlter any of these annotation inconsistencies and leave that
880,1468,1123,1468,1123,1502,880,1502,effort for future work.
880,1527,1242,1527,1242,1567,880,1567,3.3. Language Identiﬁcation
880,1581,1536,1581,1536,1613,880,1613,Although many cues for where a form ﬁeld should go are vi-
880,1613,1536,1613,1536,1648,880,1648,sual, such as an underline or empty box, there are many tex-
880,1648,1536,1648,1536,1680,880,1680,tual cues as well. Cues such as colons before blank spaces,
880,1680,1536,1680,1536,1713,880,1713,circle or check all that apply sequences, and column headers
880,1713,1536,1713,1536,1747,880,1747,all use text to indicate the presence of a form ﬁeld. There
880,1747,1536,1747,1536,1779,880,1779,is also a difference in form ﬁeld placement in right-to-left
880,1779,1536,1779,1536,1814,880,1814,and left-to-right language. As such, models trained to de-
880,1814,1536,1814,1536,1846,880,1846,tect form ﬁelds beneﬁt from a large number of examples for
880,1846,1045,1846,1045,1879,880,1879,each language.
912,1882,1534,1882,1534,1915,912,1915,We perform language identiﬁcation on C OMMON -
880,1915,1536,1915,1536,1947,880,1947,F ORMS by extracting all of the text from every page, and
880,1947,1536,1947,1536,1982,880,1982,FastText [ 12 ] to classify the likeliest language per page. We
